testsuite.name: LastPencilTest

testcase.name: start

failure.message:
java.lang.AssertionError: Wrong answer in test #16
When one of the players enters the number of pencils they want to remove, the program should print 2 non-empty lines.
Please find below the output of your program during this failed test. Note that the '>' character indicates the beginning of the input line.
---
How many pencils would you like to use: > 5 Who will be the first (John, Jack): > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > John ||||| John's turn! > 4 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > a Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 0 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > -1 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > _ Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > | Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > ||||| Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 2 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 4 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > a Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 0 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > -1 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > _ Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > | Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > ||||| Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 1 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3'

testcase.failure:
java.lang.AssertionError: Wrong answer in test #16
When one of the players enters the number of pencils they want to remove, the program should print 2 non-empty lines.
Please find below the output of your program during this failed test. Note that the '>' character indicates the beginning of the input line.
---
How many pencils would you like to use: > 5 Who will be the first (John, Jack): > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > John ||||| John's turn! > 4 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > a Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > testsuite.system-out: ?[1;31m Start test 1?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > 0 The number of pencils should be positive > a The number of pencils should be numeric > 0 The number of pencils should be positive > 1 Who will be the first (John, Jack): ?[1;31m Start test 2?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > 0 The number of pencils should be positive > 0 The number of pencils should be positive > 0 The number of pencils should be positive > 0 The number of pencils should be positive > 0 The number of pencils should be positive ?[1;31m Start test 3?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > a The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 4?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > _ The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 5?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > test The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 6?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > | The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 7?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > ||||| The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 8?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 9?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > - The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 10?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > two The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 11?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > 10g The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 12?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > k5 The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 13?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > -0.2 The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 14?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > 0.3 The number of pencils should be numeric ?[1;31m Start test 15?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > 1 Who will be the first (John, Jack): ?[1;31m Start test 16?[0m How many pencils would you like to use: > 5 Who will be the first (John, Jack): > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > JohnJack Choose between 'John' and 'Jack' > John ||||| John's turn! > 4 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > a Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 0 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > -1 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > _ Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > | Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > ||||| Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 2 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 4 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > a Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 0 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > -1 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > _ Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > | Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > ||||| Possible values: '1', '2' or '3' > 1 Possible values: '1', '2' or '3'
